title: Xtra Bouncy Canadian Fish Noodles
description: A pescatarian-friendly Canadian dish featuring gelatine leaves and bonito flakes, this recipe is a unique and tasty monsoon-inspired meal. The resinous and bouncy texture of the noodles makes it a standout dish.
instructions: Soak the gelatine leaves in cold water for 5 minutes to soften them.
Combine the noodle flour and water in a large mixing bowl to form a dough.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Divide the dough into 4 equal pieces and roll each piece into a long rope.
Cut the rope into 1-inch pieces to form the noodles.
Heat the vegetable oil in a large pan over medium heat and cook the noodles for 5 minutes.
Add the bonito flakes, soy sauce, and sesame oil to the pan and stir to combine.
Add the cod fillets to the pan and cook for an additional 5 minutes.
Drain the gelatine leaves and add them to the pan, stirring to combine.
Serve the Xtra Bouncy Canadian Fish Noodles hot, garnished with additional bonito flakes if desired.
